summary:
Anti-centromere antibodies are primarily found in limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis or CREST syndrome. They have a lower prevalence in other connective tissue diseases like lupus and Sjogrens syndrome. Given these findings, they serve as an important marker for diagnosing specific forms of scleroderma. To summarize concisely:

- Key finding: Anti-centromere antibodies predominantly indicate limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis/CREST syndrome among auto-immune disorders. This condition stands out due to its characteristic skin involvement pattern compared to others where such markers may be present at varying levels only. Sentence count: 2
The summary focuses solely on critical aspects directly related to identifying when one would encounter anti-centromere antibodies clinically—specifically linking them closely with their diagnostic utility within certain subsets of systemic sclerosis while noting differential expression patterns across various rheumatic illnesses.
